html:
The global geopolitical landscape is dominated by conflicting signals regarding US-Iran relations, with reports of continued exchanges of fire amidst attempts at diplomacy. This tension is further exacerbated by Iranian state media revealing that Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei lost his life in a US and Israeli airstrike on his office at the onset of the imposed war.

US-Iran Tensions: Conflicting reports surround potential US-Iran talks in Qatar, with President Trump suggesting an "important" meeting while Iran denies agreeing to direct negotiations, stating officials will be in Doha for discussions on frozen assets. This diplomatic uncertainty follows weekend exchanges of fire, putting a recent ceasefire in jeopardy and contributing to global energy market instability. Iranian state media reports preparations for the funeral of Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei, stating he lost his life in a US and Israeli airstrike on his office on the first day of the imposed war.

Venezuela Crisis: Venezuela grapples with a catastrophic aftermath of twin earthquakes, with over 58,000 buildings reportedly damaged or destroyed and the UN procuring "10,000 body bags" as the death toll rises to 1,719. Residents accuse the government of negligence, forcing many to fend for themselves amid the devastation. The disaster tragically claimed the lives of Venezuelans deported from the U.S. just hours after their return.

European Security: Unease deepens in Russia following a significant Ukrainian drone attack on Moscow and strikes on two Russian oil refineries overnight. Separately, a Ukrainian oligarch and family were injured by a bomb in Monaco, caused by an explosive device. In a move to bolster its defenses against Russian aggression, Poland has placed an order for three Swedish submarines.

UK Politics & Defence: Keir Starmer, whose resignation two years after taking office leaves his UK-Africa partnership ambitions unfinished, is set to unveil a long-delayed defence investment plan. The plan, which includes £5bn for drones and focuses on a "hybrid navy," faces criticism from Tories and Lib Dems for being "too little, too late."

Global Trade: The European Union has halved its duty-free steel quota from abroad, aiming to reduce cheap Chinese steel imports, though 12 free trade partners including the UK will face a smaller reduction. This comes as the EU gets tough on China amidst trade imbalance and fears of deindustrialisation. Separately, Chinese tycoon Guo Wengui was sentenced to 30 years in US jail for fraudulent schemes.

Social Unrest & Regional Security: South Africa is deploying heavy security ahead of planned anti-migrant protests, which have seen thousands leave the country ahead of a Tuesday deadline. A shooting in Stade, Germany, killed six people. In the Middle East, Israeli forces intensified military operations across Gaza, with drone strikes reportedly killing several civilians. Israeli troops also faced resistance as they pushed into a town in Syria, with reports of retaliatory helicopter strikes.
